overallVerdict: Heavy on ambition, light on receipts — the code tells a humbler story than the resume.

claims.summary: React usage is real but activity spans ~14 months, not 3 years. Component patterns are functional but lack advanced hooks.
claims.evidence: Oldest React commit found: 14 months ago in react-dashboard. No use of useCallback, useMemo, or custom hooks beyond a single useLocalStorage.

receipts.label: First React commit
receipts.detail: init: add react scaffold — 14 months ago

receipts.label: components/Dashboard.tsx
receipts.detail: useState only. No memoization, no context, no advanced patterns.

claims.whatToAskNext: Walk me through the most complex React pattern you've built and why you chose it.

claims.summary: The ml-pipeline repo is a Jupyter notebook tutorial clone with no deployment infrastructure, no data pipeline, and one dataset: iris.csv.
claims.evidence: ml-pipeline/notebook.ipynb: sklearn LinearRegression on iris.csv with accuracy printout. No CI/CD, no serving layer, no real data ingestion.

receipts.label: ml-pipeline/notebook.ipynb
receipts.detail: from sklearn.datasets import load_iris — tutorial-grade code

receipts.label: No deployment config found
receipts.detail: Zero Dockerfile, requirements.txt only lists sklearn+pandas, no serving layer

claims.whatToAskNext: How did you handle model versioning and rollback in production? What monitoring did you set up?

claims.claim: Led backend architecture for a real-time system
claims.summary: No evidence of real-time systems, websockets, or backend architecture in any indexed repository.
claims.evidence: Searched all repos for websocket, socket.io, SSE, event-driven, pubsub, kafka, redis — zero matches. No backend repos beyond a small Express hello-world.

receipts.label: hackathon-app
receipts.detail: Only backend code: 40-line Express server with 2 GET routes

claims.whatToAskNext: Which repo has the real-time system? Can you share the architecture diagram or ADR you wrote for it?

redemption: The hackathon projects are genuinely shipped and functional — this is someone who executes under pressure, which counts for a lot.
